info.title: Vox Pop Core API
info.description:
Open-source REST surface for Vox Pop — users, prompts, replies, and the auth surface that links them.

## Authentication

Every authenticated endpoint accepts a bearer token via the `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header. Two token types are accepted interchangeably:

- **Firebase ID token** — issued by the Firebase Auth client SDK. Short-lived (~1 hour); refresh client-side.
- **Firebase session cookie** — issued by the hosted dashboard via `POST /api/v1/auth/session` (apps/web only; per-origin Set-Cookie semantics). Longer-lived; useful for server-rendered clients.

Both verify against the same Firebase project. Public endpoints accept missing/invalid tokens but project to the public view shape.

## Envelope

Every JSON response wraps its payload: `{ success: true, data: T }` on success, `{ success: false, error: { message, code?, issues? }, requestId }` on failure. Pagination cursors live inside `data.nextCursor`.

tags.name: Users
tags.description: User primitives, the viewer's own profile, and public identity projections (profiles, handle resolution).

tags.name: Prompts
tags.description: Prompt primitives — create, read, update status, and the public prompt projection.

tags.name: Replies
tags.description: Reply primitives plus the cross-prompt feed and transcription search queries.

tags.name: Audio
tags.description: Audio storage primitives — the signed-URL proxy and the authenticated / anonymous upload endpoints.

tags.name: Connectors
tags.description: Connector control plane — uniform per-connector config, status, and enable/disable (settings opaque, secrets by reference).

tags.name: Organizations
tags.description: Organization primitives — create/update, membership, role management, invites, and the public org-profile projection.

get.summary: Resolve a handle to a user or organization
get.description: Public identity lookup over the unified handle space. Returns a discriminated union — `{ type: 'user', profile }` or `{ type: 'org', org }` — or `null` when the handle resolves to neither. The user projection omits PII (basic profile only).

schema.description: The handle to resolve (user handle or org slug; case-insensitive)

200.description: The resolved user or organization, or null

get.description: Paginated list of prompts authored by the authenticated viewer. Cursor-paginated by prompt id.

schema.description: Pagination cursor — the last prompt id from the prior page

200.description: Paginated list of the viewer's prompts

post.description: Creates a prompt authored by the viewer (or by the active org if `currentOrg` is set on the session). Supports the `Idempotency-Key` request header for safe retries.

schema.description: Optional idempotency key — repeated requests with the same key return the cached response.

403.description: Not a member of the active organization

409.description: Idempotency conflict — prior request still in flight

get.description: Returns the prompt. The owner sees the full `PromptView` (including author-private CRM state); non-owners and anonymous viewers see `PromptViewPublic`. Non-live prompts visible only to the owner.

delete.description: Soft-deletes the prompt (status → `deleted`). Owner or active-org member only.

patch.description: Flips a prompt to `live` or `archived`. Owner or active-org member only. Returns `data: null` on success.

patch.summary: Set the AT Protocol URI of a published prompt
patch.description: Records the `at://` URI returned by the publisher after a successful `repo.putRecord` on the author's PDS. Owner or active-org member only. Narrow-scope endpoint — only `atprotoUri` can be set this way. Returns `data: null` on success.

post.summary: Mark every reply on a prompt as read by the viewer
post.description: Adds the viewer's uid to every reply's `readBy` set for the given prompt. Idempotent. No ownership check (matches `POST /replies/:id/read`).

get.description: Returns every reply on the given prompt with owner-aware projection. The prompt author sees the full `ReplyView[]` (with CRM/PII state); everyone else sees `ReplyViewPublic[]`. Non-live prompts return `[]` for non-authors.

schema.description: When `true`, include replies whose status is `archived`. Default `false`.

get.description: Returns the full `ProfileViewSelf` (PII-included) for the authenticated viewer.

patch.description: Partial-update profile fields (display name, bio, handle, email, rssFeedUrl, etc.). Handle-swap is transactional. Returns `data: null` on success.

get.summary: List orgs the viewer belongs to
get.description: Returns hydrated `OrganizationView[]` for every org the authenticated viewer is a member of.
Returns whether `candidate` is available, taken by another user, owned by the viewer, or invalid.

post.summary: Claim or re-affirm the viewer's handle
post.description: Reserves the candidate handle for the authenticated viewer in an atomic transaction. Echoes the claimed handle + app domain on success.
Soft-deletes (deactivates) the account: marks the user doc, releases the handle, and revokes refresh tokens. Does NOT remove prompts, replies, or org memberships.
Sets the active-org custom claim for the authenticated viewer. Verifies membership in the target org before granting the claim; re-denormalizes the full org map from membership docs.

403.description: Not a member of the target organization

post.description: Resets the `new_replier` or `unread_reply` badge for the authenticated viewer.

get.description: Paginated discovery list of users with a public handle. Cursor-paginated by handle.

schema.description: Pagination cursor — the handle of the last user from the prior page

get.description: Returns every claimed handle in the system. Used by the sitemap generator.

get.summary: Get a user profile by handle
get.description: Returns the full `ProfileView` when the authenticated viewer is the profile owner; otherwise returns `ProfileViewBasic` (handle, displayName, avatarUrl, bio).
Paginated list of prompts authored by the user identified by handle (or raw UID). The owner sees live + archived; everyone else sees live only.

schema.description: The user handle (case-insensitive) or raw UID
Pagination cursor — the last prompt id from the prior page

get.summary: Get a user profile page payload
get.description: Aggregated payload for the user-profile page: the profile, every public prompt with its replies, and the repliers list.

schema.description: The user handle (case-insensitive) or raw UID

get.description: Cross-prompt feed of the authenticated viewer's replies. Reverse-chronological, cursor-paginated. Filter by prompt id, status, read state, and date range.

schema.description: Pagination cursor — the last reply id from the prior page
Scope the feed to a single prompt
Scope the feed to replies authored by this uid (the viewer's view of one person's activity)

get.summary: Search the viewer's replies by transcription
get.description: Case-insensitive transcription substring search across the authenticated viewer's replies. Filters mirror the feed endpoint plus a required `q` (min 2 chars).

schema.description: Scope search to a single prompt

get.description: Returns the public projection (`ReplyViewPublic[]`) of replies for the given prompt. Anonymous-friendly; the parent prompt's visibility + status gates non-author access.

schema.description: When `"true"`, include archived replies (only honored for owner views)

post.description: Creates a reply on a prompt. The audio source is either a pre-uploaded `audioUrl` (authenticated upload flow) or a `pendingUploadId` (embed-redirect flow). Exactly one is required.

200.description: The hydrated reply (null if hydration failed)

404.description: Pending upload not found or expired

get.description: Returns the public projection of a single reply. Owners see their own replies regardless of parent-prompt status; non-owners see only replies on live + public prompts (404 for archived/draft, 403 for live + private).

patch.description: Flips a reply's status (`live` / `archived` / `deleted`). Author of the parent prompt only.

post.summary: Mark a reply as read by the viewer
post.description: Adds the viewer's uid to the reply's `readBy` set. Idempotent; no ownership check (matches `POST /prompts/:id/read`).

patch.summary: Update private CRM notes on a reply
patch.description: Author-of-parent-prompt only. The notes live on the enrichments doc (`enrichments/replies/items/{id}`) and never appear in the public projection.

post.summary: Bulk-apply an action to a list of replies
post.description: Apply `markRead`, `archive`, `delete`, or `restore` to a batch of reply ids. Status mutations require ownership of the parent prompt; `markRead` is idempotent and unowned.

Anonymous multipart/form-data upload for the embed-redirect flow. Fields: `file` (512B–25MB; types: m4a, mp4, mpeg, webm, ogg, wav) and `promptId` (must be a live prompt). Persists a prompt-scoped pending upload and returns its `pendingId`; the authenticated `POST /api/v1/replies` later binds it to the author. Rate-limited per IP.

pendingId.description: Opaque id to pass as `?pending=` when binding the reply

400.description: Missing/oversized/undersized file, missing promptId, or unsupported type

404.description: Prompt not found or not accepting replies

post.description: Authenticated multipart/form-data upload with a single `file` field (max 25MB; types: m4a, mp4, mpeg, webm, ogg, wav). Stores under `audio/<uid>/...` and returns its URL. Anonymous embed uploads use `POST /api/v1/audio/upload-pending` instead.

audioUrl.description: Canonical storage URL of the stored object

400.description: Missing/oversized file or unsupported audio type

get.summary: Resolve audio to a signed URL
get.description: Validates the requested object path against the served prefixes (`audio/`, `prompts/`, `replies/`) and, for reply audio, that the parent prompt exists, then 302-redirects to a short-lived signed URL (~1h TTL, cached ~50m). Anonymous — public audio playback for embeds and public pages.

schema.description: REQUIRED. The canonical storage URL (or object path) of the audio to proxy. Must resolve to an object under `audio/`, `prompts/`, or `replies/`. Returns 400 if absent.

302.description: Redirect (Location header) to a time-limited signed URL for the requested object.

403.description: Object path outside the served allowlist

404.description: Parent prompt or backing object not found

get.description: Returns the authenticated viewer's `ConnectorConfigRecord` for the connector, or 404 if none is set.

schema.description: The connector to configure (allowlisted; unknown types 400).

put.summary: Create or replace the viewer's connector config
put.description: Idempotent full write of the config envelope. `settings` is an opaque, connector-specific blob; `secretRef` references a secret store (never a raw secret). Server stamps `createdAt`/`updatedAt`.

schema.description: The connector to configure (allowlisted; unknown types 400).

patch.summary: Partially update the viewer's connector config
patch.description: Merge-update config fields (`settings`, `secretRef`, `enabled`, `status`). 404 if no config exists yet.

schema.description: The connector to configure (allowlisted; unknown types 400).

delete.description: Removes the config record. Idempotent — succeeds even if none exists. Any connector-side teardown (e.g. releasing a provisioned resource) is the connector's responsibility before calling this.

schema.description: The connector to configure (allowlisted; unknown types 400).

get.description: Returns the connector-reported `status` for the viewer's config, or 404 if none is set.

schema.description: The connector to configure (allowlisted; unknown types 400).

post.description: Flip the viewer's connector `enabled` flag to `true`. 404 if no config exists yet.

schema.description: The connector to configure (allowlisted; unknown types 400).

post.description: Flip the viewer's connector `enabled` flag to `false`. 404 if no config exists yet.

schema.description: The connector to configure (allowlisted; unknown types 400).

get.description: Returns `{ user, prompt }` for the mobile app's public prompt screen. The handle is normalized (trim `@`, lowercased). Returns 404 if the prompt isn't owned by the handle's user OR isn't live — hides both ownership and existence.

200.description: User + prompt payload for the public mobile screen

get.description: Public aggregated payload for the org-profile page: org details, public (live) prompts in the org context, and an RSS summary if configured.
Public — auth optional. When authenticated, `currentUserRole` on the returned view reflects the viewer's membership.
Requires membership (owner, admin, or member).

403.description: Not a member of this organization

post.description: Admin+ only. Bypasses the email-invite flow (caller already knows the target UID).

get.summary: List prompts in the organization context
get.description: Requires membership. Paginated (cursor); `publicOnly=true` restricts to live prompts. Non-members get 403 — the public live subset is on the org-profile projection.

403.description: Not a member of this organization

get.description: Requires membership (owner, admin, or member).

403.description: Not a member of this organization

patch.description: Admin+ only. A slug rename is collision-checked against other orgs (409 on conflict).

post.description: Any authenticated user can create an org and becomes its owner. The slug is reserved transactionally; a collision returns 409.

patch.description: Admin+ only. The owner's role cannot be changed.

delete.description: Self-removal (leave) is allowed for any member; admin+ can remove others. The owner cannot be removed.

post.description: Any authenticated user can accept an invite addressed to them. Returns the new membership.

post.summary: Disconnect the linked AT Protocol identity
post.description: Removes the authenticated viewer's linked AT Protocol identity (DID + handle) from their profile. Does not revoke the PDS-side OAuth session — call your PDS's logout flow separately if needed.
